statement: US software developer employment will grow at least 3% YoY through 2026-Q2
rationale: AI augmentation raises output per developer; Jevons effect on software demand.
resolution_criteria: BLS occupational employment statistics for SOC 15-1252.

statement: Median cloud compute price per token will fall at least 40% by 2026-Q1 vs 2025-Q1
rationale: Hardware scaling and competition among frontier labs compress inference costs.
resolution_criteria: Public pricing pages from two major API providers.

statement: US paralegal headcount will decline at least 5% YoY by 2025-Q4
rationale: Document review automation substitutes routine legal research tasks.
resolution_criteria: BLS QCEW or OES release for legal support occupations.
judged_rationale: BLS data showed a 6.2% YoY decline in paralegal employment.

statement: US call-center employment will fall more than 8% in 2025
rationale: Voice AI handles tier-1 support; firms consolidate offshore and automated channels.
resolution_criteria: BLS occupational series for customer service representatives.
judged_rationale: Annual change exceeded the 8% threshold in federal labor statistics.

statement: Major economies will enter recession before 2025-Q3
rationale: Tight monetary policy and credit contraction trigger synchronized downturn.
resolution_criteria: NBER or equivalent two-quarter GDP contraction in US, EU, or UK.
judged_rationale: No G7 economy met the recession definition by the resolution date.

statement: Data-entry clerk employment will drop at least 10% YoY by 2025-Q4
rationale: OCR and LLM form extraction replace manual keying in back-office workflows.
resolution_criteria: BLS occupational employment for data entry keyers.
judged_rationale: Occupational series showed an 11% decline versus prior year.

statement: Venture capital AI funding will exceed $80B globally in 2025
rationale: Foundation-model capex and enterprise adoption sustain deal volume.
resolution_criteria: PitchBook or Crunchbase annual AI-sector funding totals.
judged_rationale: Reported annual totals remained below the $80B threshold.

statement: EU will pass binding AI employment impact reporting rules before 2026-Q2
rationale: AI Act implementation pushes member states toward workforce disclosure.
resolution_criteria: Official Journal publication of binding employment-reporting requirements.
